body:
Reviewed the certification. The RVC comes in at 64% net cost, above the 60% threshold for that tariff shift, so the axle assemblies qualify. Keep the supplier's cost breakdown on file in case CBP asks. I'll note it on the entry.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Frank

Shipped separately from the trailer, the evaporator unit classifies under 8418.69 as refrigerating equipment, not 8708. The 8708 code only applies when it's an integral part of the vehicle. I'll send the supporting reasoning.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Wendell

At a 30% volume increase your duties and fees push past the bracket your current bond covers. I'd bump the continuous bond to the next tier now rather than wait for a CBP insufficiency notice. I can file the rider this week.

— Marcus

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Kevin

Two days late is a flag but a first occurrence with a documented vendor delay is usually mitigated. Write up the cause and the corrective step you've put in place. I'll attach that to the file so we're showing reasonable care.

— Priya

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Samuel

Unused-merchandise drawback applies since the goods were exported in the same condition. Keep the import entry, proof of duty paid, and the export bill of lading tying the two together. I'll set up the claim once you send those.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Lorraine

Both SKUs fall under an active 301 list. There's no current exclusion for that subheading, but I can prepare an exclusion request. Be ready to show there's no domestic source. I'll draft it.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Bernard

The retail carton your customer sees must carry the country of origin. If the individual unit also reaches the ultimate purchaser, it needs marking too. I'll spell out which applies per SKU.

— Marcus

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Camille

Mandatory fields are the FDA product code, manufacturer, shipper, and the anticipated arrival info. List your firm as the US agent if you want filings to route through us. I'll send the field map.

— Priya

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Michael

The recon entry is due within 21 months of the underlying entry date. If the transfer-pricing study runs long we can flag it but the deadline itself isn't extendable, so let's calendar it now.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Karen

Pulled the scope ruling. Your fastener's coating and grade put it outside the order's stated scope, so it isn't covered. I'll save the ruling reference so you can show it if CBP questions the entry.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Gerald

Good call getting certainty before the contract. I'll draft the ruling request with the spec sheet and proposed classification and submit to CBP. Expect about 30 days for a response.

— Marcus

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Rachel

For goods that may be re-exported, an FTZ subzone gives you better deferral since duty is only owed if the goods enter US commerce. A bonded warehouse caps you at five years. I lean FTZ for your cold-storage flow.

— Priya

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Diane

Yes, the tooling is a dutiable assist. We apportion it across the number of units the tooling is expected to produce and add the per-unit amount to the customs value. I'll set up the apportionment schedule.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Paul

File the PSC to correct the classification and pay the duty difference. Interest runs from the original entry date, but voluntarily correcting it limits penalty exposure. I'll prepare the PSC.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Yolanda

Sending the POA renewal now. Sign and return so there's no gap in our authority to file. It's the standard CBP POA, no changes from last term.

— Marcus

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Frank

Samples under the $800 per-shipment de minimis can enter duty-free, but only one such shipment per importer per day qualifies. If you split a day's samples across entries it won't hold. I'll note the limit.

— Priya

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Wendell

Changing the actual fiber blend is legitimate tariff engineering as long as the imported good genuinely has that composition. A paper-only change won't survive a CBP lab test. Send the new spec and I'll confirm the subheading.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Kevin

We'll respond to the CF-28 with the commercial invoice, the value buildup, and any related-party support. Get me the supplier's price list and we'll have the response in well before the deadline.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Samuel

CBP requires entry records be kept for five years from the date of entry. I'd set retention at five years minimum across the board to keep it simple. I'll send a one-page retention policy.

— Marcus

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Lorraine

Reviewed the certification. The RVC comes in at 64% net cost, above the 60% threshold for that tariff shift, so the axle assemblies qualify. Keep the supplier's cost breakdown on file in case CBP asks. I'll note it on the entry.

— Priya

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Bernard

Happy to run a standing quarterly audit of your entry summaries. We'll spot-check classification, valuation, and origin claims and flag anything before CBP would. I'll scope it as a quarterly retainer.

— Jason

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Camille

Sending the exclusion renewals now so they don't lapse. Once filed you stay covered on the existing approvals. I'll confirm the effective dates back to you.

— Brandon

Thanks, that's clear. Sending over the documents you asked for so you can move it forward.

Michael
